reason: Response A is a clean, single sentence with a unified comedic logic (speed of light vs. failing when needed), which fits Haiku's tendency toward tight, conventional one-liners that read naturally. Response B uses the more stylized "Wi-Fi:" label-and-colon construction with stacked, comma-spliced clauses ("invisible thread connecting you to infinite distraction, strongest in cafés, gone the moment...") that reads as a deliberate imitation of "wittiness" rather than an actual wry one-liner, and it overreaches the requested single-line format. The genuine Haiku response is more likely the simpler, self-contained quip in A.
